bead.title: ddx work: auto-triage on review BLOCK + substantive execution failures
bead.description:
Currently when a bead hits a post-merge review BLOCK, the drain loop emits the BLOCK event, leaves the bead open, and moves to the next ready bead. The BLOCK then sits in the queue waiting for a human. Same for many substantive execution_failed cases — no automated triage.

This bead handles BLOCK + substantive failures only. Two SEPARATE beads cover related-but-distinct concerns:
- ddx-aede917d: drain pause/resume on quota exhaustion (Fizeau-side state, DDx consumes typed error)
- ddx-c6e3db02: per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After (provider stays in rotation)

Failure modes triaged HERE:
- post-merge review BLOCK → either (a) re-attempt-with-context (BLOCK reasons embedded in next prompt), (b) escalate to next tier in the S10_2 ladder, or (c) file follow-up bead capturing BLOCK reasons + mark original needs_human
- 'staging tracker: Unable to create .git/index.lock' (git contention — see  / ddx-da11a34a) → retry with backoff after lock clears
- substantive execution_failed (probe_ok+exec_fail; agent finished but produced no useful diff or wrong shape) → escalate per S10 ladder
- 'no_changes' (agent decided nothing to do) → re-evaluate against the bead AC; if still seems valid, file a clarification follow-up rather than mark already_satisfied

Triage policy is config-driven, per-failure-mode mapping in .ddx/config.yaml triage:. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier on second BLOCK, then needs_human on third.

NOT in scope: rate-limit handling (ddx-c6e3db02) and quota-pause handling (ddx-aede917d) — those are separate beads with their own infrastructure.
bead.acceptance: 1. Loop has triage step after each attempt's terminal event. 2. Per-failure-mode triage policy in .ddx/config.yaml. 3. Triage outcomes (re-attempt-with-context, escalate-tier, needs_human) align exactly with TD-NNN TriageContract (per ddx-673833f4). **No new persisted statuses introduced — all triage state lives in labels (e.g., triage:needs-human) and events per bd/br compatibility (ADR-004).** 4. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier, then add 'triage:needs-human' label. 5. Lock-error handling delegates to ddx-da11a34a's implementation; this bead does NOT duplicate retry/backoff logic. Triage policy entry for lock errors invokes the lock-bead's published API. 6. Tests: BLOCK→re-attempt, BLOCK→escalate, lock-error→delegates-to-lock-bead, no_changes→re-evaluate. 7. Out of scope: rate-limit handling, quota-pause handling, lock-retry implementation, status-enum modification. 8. needs_human stays a LABEL per current code (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:19-23) and TD's resolution. 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**

events.summary: Auto-closed by drain with only execution-evidence files (no implementation). Reopening per the no_changes pattern that auto-triage (ddx-3c154349 itself) should fix. Real work pending.
